entities.name: SOIL ACIDIFICATION
entities.description: The process of soil becoming more acidic due to natural and human activities, which limits plant growth.

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The geographic region where acid soils are being managed to reclaim livelihoods.

entities.name: PLANT GROWTH
entities.description: The biological development of plants, which is hindered by soil acidification.

entities.name: BASE ELEMENT DEFICIENCIES
entities.description: The lack of essential base elements in the soil caused by acidification.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS FIXATION
entities.description: The process by which phosphorus is bound in the soil and becomes unavailable to plants in acidic conditions.

entities.name: ALUMINUM
entities.description: A chemical element that becomes toxic in acidic soil conditions.

entities.name: MANGANESE
entities.description: A chemical element that becomes toxic in acidic soil conditions.

entities.name: HYDROGEN IONS
entities.description: Ions that contribute to soil acidity and toxicity.

entities.name: TOXICITY
entities.description: The harmful effect caused by high concentrations of Aluminum, Manganese, and Hydrogen ions in the soil.
